message:
perf(raptorq/decoder): replace Vec<usize> column-to-dense index map with adaptive DenseColIndexMap

Introduce a DenseColIndexMap enum that selects between two
representations for mapping column indices to dense matrix positions:

- Direct: a dense Vec<usize> lookup table, used when the maximum column
  index is within 8x of the unsolved column count. This is the original
  behavior and remains optimal when column indices are compact.

- SortedPairs: a sorted Vec<(usize, usize)> with binary search lookup,
  used when column indices are sparse or high-valued. This avoids
  allocating massive index arrays (potentially hundreds of megabytes)
  when only a small fraction of column indices are actually in use.

Additionally, add a two-pointer scan fast path in
sparse_first_dense_columns that avoids building an index map entirely
when the unsolved columns are already sorted, which is the common
runtime case. The fast path walks both the unsolved-columns list and
the row's sparse column indices in tandem, yielding matches in O(n+m)
without any auxiliary allocation.

New tests:
- Test for Direct representation correctness
- Test for SortedPairs representation correctness
- Test verifying the sorted fast path produces identical results to
  the index-map path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
